g.data-narration: Welcome to IoT System Management with NETCONF-YANG - the modern standard for configuring billions of IoT devices.
Managing billions of heterogeneous IoT devices requires standardized, secure protocols. NETCONF-YANG provides the answer.
g.text: Managing billions of heterogeneous IoT devices at scale requires
standardized, secure, and automated protocols. NETCONF-YANG provides
transactional configuration, hierarchical data models, and mandatory encryption.

g.data-narration: Traditional SNMP has weak security, flat rigid MIBs, no transaction support, and no rollback capability - unsuitable for IoT.
NETCONF provides the protocol while YANG defines the data structure. Together they form a complete management solution.
g.text: The data modeling language - defines what data

g.data-narration: NETCONF has four layers: Transport (SSH mandatory), Messages (XML RPC), Operations (get-config, edit-config), and Content.
Core operations include get-config, edit-config, copy-config, lock/unlock, get, commit, and close-session for device management.
YANG provides leaf (single value), leaf-list (ordered sequence), container (grouping), list (keyed entries), and choice (alternatives).
This example YANG module defines sensor configuration with device-id, sampling-interval, and reporting-threshold parameters.
g.text: Example: YANG Module for IoT Sensor
leaf sampling-interval { type uint32; units "seconds"; default 60; }

g.data-narration: YANG distinguishes config true (writable parameters) from config false (read-only operational state like temperature readings).
The five-step workflow: Device Discovery (hello exchange), Configuration Deployment (edit-config), Monitoring (get), Automated Response, and Maintenance.
This XML shows an edit-config RPC that modifies the sensor's sampling interval and reporting threshold in the candidate datastore.
g.text: Staged in candidate datastore → validated → atomically committed to running

g.data-narration: The architecture shows Management Application, NETCONF Controller, YANG Repository, Config Database, and IoT devices connected via NETCONF.
NETCONF-YANG outperforms SNMP with strong SSH security, hierarchical YANG models, transaction support, and human-readable XML encoding.
RESTCONF provides HTTP-based access using YANG models. Challenges include resource constraints, scale management, and heterogeneity.
g.text: Challenges: Resource constraints | Scale | Heterogeneity | Legacy integration

g.data-narration: For exams: Know the four NETCONF layers, YANG constructs, config true/false, five-step workflow, and architecture components.
NETCONF-YANG provides standardized, secure, transactional IoT device management, overcoming SNMP's limitations for modern deployments.
g.text: Summary: NETCONF-YANG is the modern standard for IoT management
